reasoning:
The market close price on 2026-02-15 will use the Friday, 2026-02-13 close, which is exactly $32.11. Dow recently underwent a major restructuring (4,500 jobs cut) causing large volatility: dropping to $26, rallying to $33.60, and settling at $32.11. 

For the short-term 1-week resolution (2026-02-22, resolving on Friday, 02-20), stock movements are close to a random walk with slight upward drift, although the time-series model (0.58 estimate) notes some positive seasonality. A baseline probability of 0.53 is assigned for this date reflecting slight positive P(increase). 

For the 1-month resolution (2026-03-17), Dow has declared a $0.35 quarterly dividend with a record date of February 27, 2026. Under T+1 settlement rules, the ex-dividend date is the same as the record date (Feb 27). This mechanical $0.35 drop in price (a ~1.1% headwind) occurs between the two forecast dates. Over a 1-month period, the 1.1% dividend drop outpaces average market returns (usually ~0.8% a month), introducing negative drag on P(price > $32.11). Therefore, the probability drops to 0.49 for the second date. This perfectly reflects both fundamental market mechanics and the requirement that distant probabilities tend closer to 0.5 (0.49 is 0.01 away from 0.5, whereas 0.53 is 0.03 away).
